title: Neuropathic foot symptom documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
assessment.rationale: Supports bilateral diabetic-foot examination and clinician risk context for neuropathic foot symptoms; no decision is generated.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
source.setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician examination of both feet, neuropathy, ischaemia, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot context, risk classification already assigned, and foot-review documentation. It does not auto-classify risk or generate referral or treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

assessment.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
assessment.rationale: Supports neuropathic-pain assessment and review for neuropathic foot symptoms; no decision is generated.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
source.population: Adults with neuropathic pain managed in non-specialist settings.
source.setting: Non-specialist assessment, treatment review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult neuropathic-pain review and treatment monitoring; partial for a generic neuropathy workflow and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

sources_used.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
